metadata.title: Receipt Extraction Pipeline: Schema-First vs. No-Schema

beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. Without a schema, you're not extracting data — you're hoping for consistency.

Receipt extraction without a schema produces output — but field names vary, dates come in mixed formats, totals and subtotals get confused, and ambiguous cases get resolved silently with no indication a decision was made. The schema is not a constraint; it's a contract that makes the output auditable.

The tools this video covers were built for enterprises with engineering teams and compliance budgets. The musician in the van at 1 a.m. has access to the exact same machinery, for free, through a browser. The gap between knowing this exists and knowing how to use it is what this channel closes — one working piece of your promotion deck at a time.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: Extract from this receipt: vendor as string, date in ISO 8601, total amount as float, line items as a list, currency as 3-letter code. If any field is ambiguous or missing, set it to null and add to exception flags.

props.command: claude "Extract from this receipt: vendor (string), date (ISO 8601), total_amount (float), line_items (list of {description, quantity, unit_price}), currency (3-letter code). If any field is ambiguous or missing, set it to null and add to exception_flags list." < receipt.txt

beats.narration_text: The extraction script runs two passes: no-schema then schema-driven, and produces a side-by-side comparison highlighting divergent cells.

props.title: extract_receipts.py

beats.narration_text: 5 receipts processed. No-schema: 3 date format inconsistencies, 2 total/subtotal confusions, 0 exception flags raised. Schema-driven: dates all ISO 8601, totals correctly separated, 3 exception flags raised — one foreign currency, one handwritten total, one ambiguous line item. The schema did not just improve the output — it made the uncertainty visible.
Add exception rule: if total is over 500 dollars, flag for human review.

props.command: claude "Re-extract with this additional rule: if total_amount > 500, add high_value_flag: true to the output." < receipts_batch.txt

beats.narration_text: Two receipts above the 500-dollar threshold: both get high_value_flag true in structured output. Downstream automation routes them to human approval queue. The AI does not approve high-value receipts — it flags them and stops. The exception rule moved the accountability where it belongs.

The schema is an interface contract for extraction. Without it, ambiguities are silently resolved by the model. With it, ambiguities surface as exception flags — which is the only outcome that preserves human accountability for the decisions.
Next: audit your workspace permissions — which access does each task actually need?
Musinique. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.
